MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT LIZABETH HILL's Motion For Relief From Order To Deem Facts Admitted.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Laura M. D'Anna, Esq., of Patenaude & Felix, APC, (818) 784-3366, for Plaintiff.

Lizabeth Hill, in propria persona, Defendant.

The parties stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem deciding the subject matter. The parties and Judge Pro Tem (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to execute the formal stipulation on their behalf.

The matter is argued/discussed. There is additional discussion regarding ongoing service issues to Defendant via USPS mail. The court queries whether the parties agree to service via email, moving forward. The parties agree to the court's suggestion and defendant confirms her email address on the record. At conclusion of argument and discussion, the court adopts its tentative ruling and advises that an order shall be prepared and issued by the court, shortly.

Judge Pro Tem: John-Paul Deol. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Apr-29-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'s MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar. Per CCP 439(a), the parties are to meet and confer prior to the filing of a motion for judgment on the pleadings. Despite plaintiff's good faith efforts, defendant has not complied with the code. The court orders defendant to comply with the code. Parties shall immediately meet and confer and if appropriate, plaintiff can re-calendar this motion by May 31, 2024. The court notes that defendant calendared a motion for relief from the order deeming facts admitted which, if granted, will moot this motion.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
